original_hypothesis.title: Genesis.onGenesisSuccess Repeatable Invocation Overwrites Unclaimed Token Distributions
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Genesis.onGenesisSuccess lacks a terminal success state flag. After the first successful call (agentTokenAddress set, claimableAgentTokens populated), the function can be called again by FACTORY_ROLE. The second call passes all modifiers (whenNotCancelled, whenNotFailed, whenEnded) and the refundUserCountForFailed==0 check. Since claimableAgentTokens uses `=` assignment (not `+=`), the second call overwrites unclaimed allocations with new values. OPERATION_ROLE can redirect agent tokens from legitimate users to attacker-controlled addresses.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The absence of a success-terminal guard combined with overwrite (not accumulate) semantics creates a code-level vulnerability. While exploitation requires OPERATION_ROLE, this is a code bug — the function should not be callable twice.
preconditions: OPERATION_ROLE calls onGenesisSuccess a second time
Users from first distribution have not yet claimed
Second call provides different distribution amounts

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: First call distributes fairly. Second call sets allocations to 0 or redirects to controlled addresses.
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker-controlled addresses receive agent tokens meant for legitimate participants

economic_questions: What is the typical delay between distribution and user claims?
How much value is at risk in agent tokens per genesis event?
Is there any off-chain monitoring for duplicate calls?
validation_requirements: Confirm second onGenesisSuccess call succeeds after first

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: All unclaimed agent tokens from genesis

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Targeted theft of specific genesis allocations

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All genesis participants who haven't claimed
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Complete loss of agent token allocation
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire agent token distribution of affected genesis

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
